title: Kth Smallest Element in a BST
pattern: Tree â€” Inorder Traversal (Sorted Order Property)
coreIdea: Find the kth smallest element by exploiting the BST property that an inorder traversal visits nodes in sorted ascending order; simply stop the traversal after visiting k nodes.
recognitionCues: asks for kth smallest/rank-based element, which maps directly to inorder position
approach: Perform inorder traversal (left, node, right), maintaining a counter. Each time a node is visited, decrement/increment the counter; when the counter reaches k, that node's value is the answer. Can be done recursively with early termination or iteratively with an explicit stack for better space efficiency on early stopping.
